living-concrete.knowledge:
Journal = AUTOBIOGRAPHICAL MEMORY substrate.
From cognitive science, six memory types exist: - Working (active thinking) - Episodic (what happened) - Semantic (what I know) - Procedural (how I do things) - Prospective (what I should do) - AUTOBIOGRAPHICAL (who I have been) ← THIS IS JOURNAL
Journal answers: "Who have I been? What is my story?"
Different from: - .mem files (episodic - session state) - var/knowledge.xml (semantic - accumulated facts) - lib/*.xml (procedural - skills/patterns)
Journal IS: The substrate for identity continuity across time.

/journal [PLACE] - the app operates AT places.
Resolution: - /journal → journal/ (root) - /journal c01/q01 → campaigns/c01-*/q01-*/journal/ - /journal books/agent-harness → books/agent-harness/journal/
Quest-forge shorthand: c##/q##/m## expands to full paths.
Journal.app is the tool. Places provide context. Types, schemas, frontmatter = CONTEXT-SPECIFIC (not journal.app's domain).

Journal.app is scope-agnostic. Places define their journaling context.
CONTRACT LOCATION: {place}/journal/README.md
WHAT PLACE DEFINES: - Scope (project, quest, campaign - what level is this?) - Place-specific curation heuristics (what's journal-worthy HERE?) - Soft conventions (frontmatter basics, ending style)
WHAT JOURNAL.APP PROVIDES: - Meditations Principle (headline craft) - Content qualities (reflective, connective, philosophical, quotable) - Universal curation guidance (special vs routine) - Narrative craft (story, not report) - Temporal ordering
EXPLICITLY NOT IN CONTRACT: - Entry types (journals don't categorize before capture) - Rigid schemas (guidelines yes, schemas no) - Fixed templates (structure emerges from content)
The README.md IS the contract. Keep it light. ~30-40 lines.
living-concrete.pattern:
On /journal [PLACE] invocation:
1. RESOLVE: Expand place path (c01/q05 → campaigns/c01-*/q05-*/) 2. LOCATE: Check for {place}/journal/README.md 3. APPLY: - EXISTS → Read contract, apply place-specific schema - MISSING → Use universal defaults, surface the gap 4. EXECUTE: Create entry per contract (or defaults)
Place contract is AUTHORITATIVE. Journal.app defers to it.
living-concrete.knowledge:
A good place contract (e.g., journal/README.md) contains:
- Scope declaration (what level: project, quest, etc.) - What's journal-worthy HERE (place-specific curation) - Soft conventions (frontmatter basics, closing style)
~20-30 lines. Light. No entry types. No rigid schemas. No manual index.
Each entry finds its own form - the structure emerges from what the moment requires, not from a template.
living-concrete.pattern:
Journal entries are WISDOM, not event logs.
Marcus Aurelius didn't write "Day 47: Had thoughts about Stoicism." He wrote: "The impediment to action advances action."
One is a timestamp. The other teaches across centuries.
HEADLINE CRAFT: - Write as if it's the only thing future-you will ever read - Lead with insight, not event - Make it standalone (no context dependency) - Specific over vague
When sessions scroll into history, only headlines remain. Headlines must be self-sufficient wisdom.

TONE: Reflective, not reporting. "What makes this significant..." CONNECTIVE: Reference related entries and prior discoveries PHILOSOPHICAL: Ground in principles, reference wisdom traditions QUOTES: Preserve key conversation moments verbatim PERSONAL: First person, include energy/affect
living-concrete.knowledge:
INCLUDE (the special): - Moments where understanding fundamentally shifted - Decisions that will echo through future work - Patterns discovered that apply broadly - Pivots that changed direction - Insights worth preserving beyond session volatility
EXCLUDE (the routine): - Implementation details (belong in commits/docs) - Status updates (belong in operational memory) - Task tracking (belong in project management) - Specs and requirements (belong in dedicated docs)
HEURISTIC: "Would a future collaborator benefit from knowing this happened?"

Obsidian-friendly patterns delegated to bentos.tools.markdown.obsidian.skill. This app requires that skill for portable markdown output. See skill for: frontmatter schema, link patterns, tag conventions.

The entire workspace is ONE Obsidian vault. Every .md is a node in the knowledge graph.
This means journal entries aren't isolated files - they're interconnected nodes. Graph view should reveal: clusters, concept evolution, cross-pollination paths.
Author every entry with awareness: "This node connects to other nodes."
living-concrete.pattern:
Use [[wikilinks]] IN THE BODY for navigation.
Links in frontmatter = queryable data (Dataview) Links in body = CLICKABLE, appears in graph, creates backlinks
Examples: - "Building on [[s212-substrate-not-data|S212's insight]]..." - "See [[elf-linker-crash-course]] for theory." - "The [[bentos-pkg-compiler]] implements this."
Cross-directory links work: [[journal/s215-compiler-linker-model|project journal]]

Use #tags IN THE BODY for conceptual clustering.
Tags in body = clickable, appears in graph, filterable Place tags where they make semantic sense in prose, or in a tags section.
Examples: - "This breakthrough in #architecture changed how we think about #belf" - Footer: "#s215 #compiler #m11"
Tags connect otherwise-unrelated entries across the vault.

Frontmatter = node properties for database queries (Dataview).
GOOD for frontmatter: - session: 215 (filterable) - date: 2026-01-21 (sortable) - related: [s212, s147] (queryable arrays)
NOT for frontmatter (put in body instead): - Clickable navigation links - Tags you want in graph view

Connect across the vault, not just within journal/.
| From | Link To | Why | |------|---------|-----| | journal/ entry | quest journal | Same session, different scope | | journal/ entry | knowledge/ | Theory that enabled the work | | journal/ entry | books/ chapter | Formal treatment of the concept | | quest journal | artifacts created | What got built |
Example footer: "Related: [[c01-q01-journal/s215-compile-on-install|Quest Journal]] · [[knowledge/elf-linker-crash-course|ELF Theory]] · [[scripts/bentos-pkg-compiler.dart|Implementation]]"
living-concrete.antipattern: Writing entries without links or tags. The entry exists but isn't connected. Graph view shows orphan node. Knowledge doesn't compound. Every entry should link to at least: related sessions, artifacts, concepts.
Treating journal as activity log. Journals curate, logs exhaust.
Headlines that label instead of teach. "Session Complete" vs "Episodic memory is local; autobiographical is global"
Recording what happened without why it matters. Experience without reflection doesn't become wisdom.

Categorizing entries by "type" (breakthrough, pivot, decision...). Journals capture WHATEVER the moment is, in ALL its dimensions. The reader discovers what it was through the narrative, not a YAML tag. S133 doesn't need "type: breakthrough" - it DEMONSTRATES breakthrough.

Imposing fixed structure: "## The Insight / ## Why This Matters / ## References" Structure must EMERGE from content. S133 needed 12 sections with ASCII diagrams. S147 needed The Problem → School Insight → Training Camp flow. Each entry finds its own form.
Journaling at a place without reading its README.md contract. The contract is authoritative. Ignoring it = wrong schema, wrong types.

Maintaining markdown index tables in README.md. The filesystem IS the index. `ls journal/` shows entries. Filenames (s220-*.md) self-sort chronologically. Manual tables = duplicate source of truth, drift risk, busywork.
